data_sources: Public web sources, CoinMarketCap, Messari, DefiLlama, Stablecoin Insider, governance forums

tvl_metrics.tvl_note: TVL down ~$130M since early March 2026 to $6.66B; significant decline from ~$16B peak in late 2025
tvl_metrics.susde_supply_billions: est. 0.8-1.2 (derived from higher sUSDe APY vs sUSDe spot)
tvl_metrics.tvl_composition_note: TVL = USDe + sUSDe + USDtb total supply in USD equivalent

revenue_drivers: Perpetual futures funding rates (short position collateral)
ETH staking yield from LST backing
revenue_metrics.critical_risk: Revenue is funding-rate dependent. Negative funding rates (present in April 2026) compress USDe yield to ~3.5% APY, driving redemptions and TVL contraction

valuation_metrics.valuation_note: FDV ~$1.63B assumes full dilution at 15B max supply; token still under vesting (unlocks continue until April 2027)

fee_switch_status.activation_requirement_2: Cumulative protocol revenue > $250M (MET - accumulated >$250M by Sept 2025)
fee_switch_status.activation_requirement_3: USDe on 4+ of top 5 CEX derivatives (MET)
fee_switch_status.governance_status: Risk Committee sign-off on implementation details pending; community vote required
fee_switch_status.expected_activation_timeline: Q1-Q2 2026 (based on governance pace, not yet confirmed live)
fee_switch_status.real_yield_aprox_pct_if_activated: 4.5-15% APR for sENA (varies with revenue levels)
fee_switch_status.real_yield_model_assumption: At current $65M quarterly revenue (~$260M annualized), 50% allocation to sENA = ~$130M/year; distributed over ~$750M staked ENA = ~17% APR theoretical, but actual is lower due to governance conservatism

real_yield_analysis.sena_real_yield_status: NOT YET LIVE - fee switch awaiting final implementation
real_yield_analysis.sena_real_yield_note: Fee switch parameters met in Sept 2025, but as of April 2026 governance has not completed Risk Committee approval + community vote. Yield will only flow once fee switch is formally activated.
real_yield_analysis.inflationary_yield_note: No emissions to sENA (all ENA minting is to the protocol operating treasury and staking reserves, not dilution)
real_yield_analysis.usde_holder_yield_status: COMPRESSED due to negative funding rates in April 2026
real_yield_analysis.usde_yield_comparison: Down from ~11% annualized in bull conditions to 3.5% in current bearish environment; sUSDe yields ~5.06% APY (Llama data)
real_yield_analysis.yield_sustainability_risk: CRITICAL - heavily dependent on positive funding rates on BTC/ETH perpetuals. When funding is negative (as in April 2026), reserve fund absorbs losses; users do not see negative returns, but yield collapses toward 0%

comparable_protocols.name: Maker/Sky
comparable_protocols.model: Real yield from DAI stability fees and real-world asset yields

comparable_protocols.name: Frax Finance
comparable_protocols.model: Algorithmic stablecoin with restaking and yield strategies

comparable_protocols.name: Liquity
comparable_protocols.model: Liquidation fees and stability pool yields

peer_comparison.ethena_differentiation: Unlike Maker/Sky (RWA yield) or Frax (restaking), Ethena's yield is primarily derivative funding—high when leverage is bullish, collapses during bear markets. Unique structural risk.

key_risks.description: Revenue is almost entirely dependent on positive funding rates in perpetual futures markets. Negative funding (April 2026 present state) collapses protocol revenue and USDe yield to ~0%, forcing redemptions and TVL contraction.
key_risks.impact: HIGH - Direct impact to real yield for sENA holders; TVL down $130M in March alone
key_risks.mitigation: Reserve fund designed to absorb negative periods; users protected from negative yields but yield becomes 0%

key_risks.description: TVL has contracted from $16B (late 2025) to $6.66B (April 2026) due to yield compression. $1.6B in USDe redemptions occurred as funding rates turned negative.
key_risks.impact: HIGH - Reduces fee revenue available to distribute to sENA holders
key_risks.mitigation: None structural; depends on market sentiment and funding rate recovery

key_risks.description: Fee switch parameters were met in September 2025, but as of April 2026 activation is still pending Risk Committee implementation sign-off and community governance vote. sENA holders have not yet received real yield.
key_risks.impact: MEDIUM - Governance uncertainty; delayed tokenomics realization
key_risks.mitigation: Governance processes underway; activation expected within 1-2 quarters

key_risks.description: KelpDAO exploit (April 18, 2026) and broader synthetic stablecoin skepticism are driving capital toward established stablecoins (USDC, USDT) over USDe.
key_risks.impact: MEDIUM - Marginal pressure on USDe demand and TVL
key_risks.mitigation: Ethena has no direct exposure to KelpDAO; risk is sentiment-driven

summary_3_line_funding_dependency_risk: Ethena's revenue model is structurally dependent on positive funding rates in BTC/ETH perpetual futures markets—when basis turns negative (as in April 2026), protocol revenue collapses and USDe yield drops from 11% to 3.5% APY, triggering $1.6B in redemptions and TVL contraction from $16B to $6.66B. Real yield to sENA holders (currently 0%, pending fee switch activation) will only materialize when funding rates are positive; during bear markets, sENA holders may see 0% yield despite the fee switch being live. This funding-rate dependency is fundamentally distinct from RWA-backed stablecoins (Maker/Sky) and makes Ethena yield highly cyclical and market-regime dependent.

sources.title: ENA Market Cap and Supply Data
USDe Funding Rate and Yield Analysis
